sentenceBreakdowns: Anh (he) + thì (as for) + thích (like) + cơm (rice) + nóng (hot)
Chị (she) + thì (as for) + uống (drink) + trà (tea) + đá (ice) + mỗi (every) + ngày (day)
Em (I) + thì (as for) + ăn (eat) + mì (noodles) + gói (instant)

translation.en: If you go, then I go.

exampleSentencesTranslated: As for him, he likes hot rice.
As for her, she drinks iced tea every day.
As for me, I eat instant noodles.
notes:
thì means “as for” and marks the topic you want to contrast or highlight. It helps shift focus, like “speaking of…” or “when it comes to…”.

It often follows a noun or pronoun to set up a comment about that person/thing.  

It’s also very common in conditional sentences to mean “then.”  

Think of thì as a spotlight word — it points to what you’re about to say next.
notes_md:
**thì** means “as for” and marks the topic you want to contrast or highlight. It helps shift focus, like “speaking of…” or “when it comes to…”.

It often follows a noun or pronoun to set up a comment about that person/thing.  

It’s also very common in conditional sentences to mean “then.”  

Think of **thì** as a spotlight word — it points to what you’re about to say next.

translation.en: If it rains, then stay home.

definitions.vi: Từ dùng để nối hai mệnh đề, thường chỉ điều kiện hoặc thời gian.
definitions.en: A word used to connect clauses, often indicating condition or time.

example.en: If it rains, we will stay at home.
example.vi: Nếu trời mưa thì chúng ta sẽ ở nhà.

exampleSentences: Chị thì uống trà đá mỗi ngày.
